     === is .murder tracked at all? ===

● .murder/plans/ is gitignored (runtime artifacts), so no per-file history. The schema answer
  is decisive though: PlanStatus has only ever had draft/accepted/superseded since the
  initial scaffold — open and active were never valid in this codebase. Let me confirm the
  writer can't emit them and look at the full frontmatter.

● Bash(cd /home/user/Documents/code/murder
      echo "=== parser parse/render/write ==="; sed -n '1,200p' murder/work/plans/parser.py)
  ⎿  === parser parse/render/write ===
     """Plan markdown parser/writer."""
     … +109 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Let me check whether conflict has any resolution path in the current pipeline (or whether
  it's a sticky terminal state).

● Bash(cd /home/user/Documents/code/murder
      echo "=== full tui-polish frontmatter ==="; sed -n '1,14p'
      .murder/plans/plan-tui-polish-cleanup.md…)
  ⎿  === full tui-polish frontmatter ===
     ---
     created_at: '2026-05-30T00:00:00'
     … +54 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

● Traced it end to end. Short answer: the two parse_errors are bad-data-origin (never
  producible by the pipeline itself), but the one conflict is fully reachable by the current
  pipeline — and it's a sticky terminal state. Details:

  parse_error → external/hand edits, not a schema migration

  The pipeline's writer can't produce these. render() (parser.py:64-73) always emits status:
  plan.status.value, which is by construction a valid enum member. The only way a file gets
  an invalid status: is if something other than the pipeline wrote it. And it's not a case of
  "we renamed enum values and old files went stale" — PlanStatus has been exactly
  draft/accepted/superseded since the initial scaffold (2e8fbf3); open and active were never
  valid here.

  The two files confirm external authorship:

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
❯ 
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) · esc to interrupt
